L. 109–59section 10101 of this titlesection 13102 of title 49

children.heading: .—Board
children.label: 1 .—Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Surface Transportation Board.

children.heading: .—Broker
children.label: 2 .—Broker
children.text: The term “broker” means a person, other than a motor carrier or an employee or agent of a motor carrier, that as a principal or agent sells, offers for sale, negotiates for, or holds itself out by solicitation, advertisement, or otherwise as selling, providing, or arranging for, transportation by motor carrier for compensation.

children.heading: .—Carrier
children.label: 3 .—Carrier
children.text: The term “carrier” means a motor carrier, a water carrier, and a freight forwarder.

children.label: A
children.text: for transportation provided before , service provided pursuant to a permit issued under section 10923, as in effect on ; andJanuary 1, 1996December 31, 1995

children.label: B
children.text: for transportation provided after , service provided under an agreement entered into under section 14101(b).December 31, 1995

children.label: A
children.text: common directors, officers, stockholders, a voting trust, or a holding or investment company, or

children.label: B
children.text: any other means.

children.label: i
children.text: that is domiciled in a contiguous foreign country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that is owned or controlled by persons of a contiguous foreign country; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a person that is not a motor carrier of property, that provides interstate transportation of property by motor vehicle under an agreement or contract entered into with a motor carrier of property (other than a motor private carrier or a motor carrier of property described in subparagraph (A)).

children.label: i
children.text: that is domiciled in a contiguous foreign country; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that is owned or controlled by persons of a contiguous foreign country; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a person that is not a motor private carrier, that provides interstate transportation of property by motor vehicle under an agreement or contract entered into with a person (other than a motor carrier of property or a motor private carrier described in subparagraph (A)).

children.label: A
children.text: assembles and consolidates, or provides for assembling and consolidating, shipments and performs or provides for break-bulk and distribution operations of the shipments;

children.label: B
children.text: assumes responsibility for the transportation from the place of receipt to the place of destination; and

children.label: C
children.text: uses for any part of the transportation a carrier subject to jurisdiction under this subtitle.

children.heading: .—Highway
children.label: 9 .—Highway
children.text: The term “highway” means a road, highway, street, and way in a State.

children.label: A
children.text: arranged and paid for by the householder, except such term does not include property moving from a factory or store, other than property that the householder has purchased with the intent to use in his or her dwelling and is transported at the request of, and the transportation charges are paid to the carrier by, the householder; or

children.label: B
children.text: arranged and paid for by another party.

children.heading: .—Household goods freight forwarder
children.label: 11 .—Household goods freight forwarder
children.text: The term “household goods freight forwarder” means a freight forwarder of one or more of the following items: household goods, unaccompanied baggage, or used automobiles.

children.label: i
children.text: Binding and nonbinding estimates.

children.label: ii
children.text: Inventorying.

children.label: iii
children.text: Protective packing and unpacking of individual items at personal residences.

children.label: iv
children.text: Loading and unloading at personal residences.

children.heading: .—Inclusion
children.label: B .—Inclusion
children.text: The term includes any person that is considered to be a household goods motor carrier under regulations, determinations, and decisions of the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration that are in effect on the date of enactment of the Household Goods Mover Oversight Enforcement and Reform Act of 2005.

children.heading: .—Limited service exclusion
children.label: C .—Limited service exclusion
children.text: The term does not include a motor carrier when the motor carrier provides transportation of household goods in containers or trailers that are entirely loaded and unloaded by an individual (other than an employee or agent of the motor carrier).

children.label: A
children.text: is the shipper, consignor, or consignee of a household goods shipment;

children.label: B
children.text: is identified as the shipper, consignor, or consignee on the face of the bill of lading;

children.label: C
children.text: owns the goods being transported; and

children.label: D
children.text: pays his or her own tariff transportation charges.

children.heading: .—Motor carrier
children.label: 14 .—Motor carrier
children.text: The term “motor carrier” means a person providing motor vehicle transportation for compensation.

children.label: A
children.text: the transportation is as provided in ;section 13501 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the person is the owner, lessee, or bailee of the property being transported; and

children.label: C
children.text: the property is being transported for sale, lease, rent, or bailment or to further a commercial enterprise.

children.heading: .—Motor vehicle
children.label: 16 .—Motor vehicle
children.text: The term “motor vehicle” means a vehicle, machine, tractor, trailer, or semitrailer propelled or drawn by mechanical power and used on a highway in transportation, or a combination determined by the Secretary, but does not include a vehicle, locomotive, or car operated only on a rail, or a trolley bus operated by electric power from a fixed overhead wire, and providing local passenger transportation similar to street-railway service.

children.heading: .—Noncontiguous domestic trade
children.label: 17 .—Noncontiguous domestic trade
children.text: The term “noncontiguous domestic trade” means transportation subject to jurisdiction under chapter 135 involving traffic originating in or destined to Alaska, Hawaii, or a territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—Person
children.label: 18 .—Person
children.text: The term “person”, in addition to its meaning under , includes a trustee, receiver, assignee, or personal representative of a person.section 1 of title 1

children.heading: .—Pre-arranged ground transportation service
children.label: 19 .—Pre-arranged ground transportation service
children.text: The term “pre-arranged ground transportation service” means transportation for a passenger (or a group of passengers) that is arranged in advance (or is operated on a regular route or between specified points) and is provided in a motor vehicle with a seating capacity not exceeding 15 passengers (including the driver).

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 20 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 21 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means the 50 States of the United States and the District of Columbia.

children.label: A
children.text: is licensed as a taxicab by a State or a local jurisdiction; or

children.label: i
children.text: provides local transportation for a fare determined (except with respect to transportation to or from airports) primarily on the basis of the distance traveled; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not primarily provide transportation to or from airports.

children.label: A
children.text: a motor vehicle, vessel, warehouse, wharf, pier, dock, yard, property, facility, instrumentality, or equipment of any kind related to the movement of passengers or property, or both, regardless of ownership or an agreement concerning use; and

children.label: B
children.text: services related to that movement, including arranging for, receipt, delivery, elevation, transfer in transit, refrigeration, icing, ventilation, storage, handling, packing, unpacking, and interchange of passengers and property.

children.heading: .—United states
children.label: 24 .—United states
children.text: The term “United States” means the States of the United States and the District of Columbia.

children.heading: .—Vessel
children.label: 25 .—Vessel
children.text: The term “vessel” means a watercraft or other artificial contrivance that is used, is capable of being used, or is intended to be used, as a means of transportation by water.

children.heading: .—Water carrier
children.label: 26 .—Water carrier
children.text: The term “water carrier” means a person providing water transportation for compensation.

children.heading: .—Over-the-road bus
children.label: 27 .—Over-the-road bus
children.text: The term “over-the-road bus” means a bus characterized by an elevated passenger deck located over a baggage compartment.
